title: Xenial Garlicky Norwegian Lamb Stew
description: A hearty and nostalgic Norwegian dish featuring tender lamb, garlicky flavors, and a hint of chervil, finished to an al-dente texture, and Whole30-friendly.
instructions: In a large Dutch oven, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium-high heat.
Add the lamb cubes and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the lamb from the pot and set aside, then add the sliced onion and cook until translucent.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly.
Add the sliced carrots and celery and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the lamb broth, browned lamb, chopped chervil, sea salt, and black pepper to the pot.
Bring the stew to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 2.5 hours.
Check the stew for tenderness and seasoning, adjusting as needed.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with additional chervil if desired.
